{"options": ["They prevent Lovable from making any changes at all", "They are required for all prompts", "They make the fix process slower", "They keep fixes focused and prevent breaking working features"], "question": "What's the purpose of including guardrails (\"don't modify X\") in debugging prompts?", "isCorrect": true, "explanation": "Guardrails keep fixes focused on the problem area. This is especially important when escaping bug loops!", "selectedAnswer": "They keep fixes focused and prevent breaking working features"}

{"options": ["Only what is broken", "What you clicked and what error appeared", "Specific location, expected behaviour, actual behaviour, and clues found", "A long description of the entire app"], "question": "Which elements make up an effective debugging prompt?", "isCorrect": true, "explanation": "The most effective prompts clearly state what you did exactly where in your app, what you expected, what actually happened, and any clues you found.
